steps.title: Convert line l to slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Starting with $3y + 12x = 5$, I'll isolate $y$ to reveal the slope.

$$3y = -12x + 5$$

$$y = -4x + \frac{5}{3}$$

The slope of line $l$ is $-4$.

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular slope relationship
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals: if one has slope $m$, the other has slope $-\frac{1}{m}$.

Line $l$ has slope $-4$, so line $n$ has slope:

$$m_n = -\frac{1}{-4} = \frac{1}{4}$$

The slope of line $n$ is $\frac{1}{4}$ or \$0.25$.
